systems.title: Gridsz Automation
systems.subtitle: AI-Powered Ticket Automation for Field Service Operations

caseStudy.problem: Field technicians working on fiber network installations received dozens of daily work orders through the Gridsz platform. Manually downloading tickets, organizing attachments (photos, diagrams, documents), and tracking status changes consumed 2-3 hours of administrative work per day.
constraints: Target platform frequently updates UI, breaking traditional selectors
Mixed attachment types requiring intelligent categorization
50+ attachments per ticket with unreliable network
Long-running automation (30+ min) needed progress visibility
No external API costs—all AI must run locally
caseStudy.approach: Built an intelligent end-to-end automation system using local LLM (Ollama) for dynamic UI element detection instead of brittle CSS selectors, YOLO11 for automatic image classification, transactional download logic with SHA256 deduplication, and WebSocket-based real-time progress streaming.
outcomes: Admin time: 2-3 hours → 15 minutes oversight (85% reduction)
Zero missed attachments—every file captured and classified
Consistent folder structure for field access
Hourly sync during work hours with automatic archival

challenges.title: Dynamic UI Element Detection
challenges.problem: Target platform frequently updates UI, breaking traditional CSS selectors—the #1 cause of maintenance burden in web automation.
challenges.solution: Intent-based AI selector system using local LLM (Ollama). DOM Snapshot compression reduces 200KB+ HTML to ~2KB candidates. LLM proposes selectors with confidence scores, validated against live page, cached for 60-min TTL.

challenges.title: LLM Latency in Automation Loops
challenges.problem: Calling LLM for every element lookup added 1-2 seconds per interaction, making automation slow.
challenges.solution: Multi-layer caching: URL pattern + intent + DOM hash as key, 60-min TTL, 70-80% hit rate. Average element lookup dropped from 1.5s to 0.3s.

challenges.title: Unreliable File Downloads
challenges.problem: Some tickets have 50+ attachments. Network interruptions caused incomplete downloads and duplicates.
challenges.solution: Transactional download logic (all succeed or none committed), SHA256 deduplication, SQLite tracking, exponential backoff retry with 3 attempts.

challenges.title: Mixed Image Types
challenges.problem: Field technicians needed to quickly identify FTU installation photos vs floor plans vs miscellaneous images.
challenges.solution: Custom-trained YOLO11 classification model: 3 categories (FTU, Drawing, Other) + location sub-classification. 87% accuracy, 11MB model size, runs locally.

challenges.title: Real-time Progress Visibility
challenges.problem: Long-running automation (30+ minutes) left users uncertain about progress.
challenges.solution: WebSocket-based streaming: progress percentage, live log entries, ticket completion notifications, auto-reconnect on connection loss.
